title: Jacquemontia parviflora
taxonomy: Jacquemontia parviflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. This family consists of plants commonly known as morning glories or bindweeds. Jacquemontia parviflora is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and its classification further breaks down into the genus Jacquemontia and the kingdom Plantae. As a member of the Convolvulaceae family, Jacquemontia parviflora is part of the order Solanales and the clade Angiosperms. This plant is also known as the small-flowered Jacquemontia or the small-flowered morning glory.

intro: Jacquemontia parviflora, also known as the small-flowered Jacquemontia or Creeping Jacquemontia, is a perennial herbaceous vine native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Jacquemontia parviflora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or used in infusions, and it is also used to make a topical paste or salve for wound healing and skin conditions.
